description: Server-side prototype pollution — black-box detection across Express, Fastify, Hapi, Koa, NestJS. Standalone from prototype_pollution.json (which is the broad/client-side reference). This catalog targets the gadgets PortSwigger's 2023 server-side-proto-pollution lab covers + the v8/Node execArgv RCE chain.

express_default_property_pollution.description: Express deeply-merging req.body into a target object. Pollution of `status` / `statusCode` / `json spaces` Express response defaults observable via altered behavior across unrelated endpoints.

probes.description: Pollute Express json-spaces; subsequent JSON responses gain 4-space indentation
Pollute response.status default. Next API call returns HTTP 510 with no business logic firing

constructor_prototype_gadget.description: `__proto__` filtered, but `constructor.prototype.X` walks the same prototype chain. Always test both forms when one is rejected.

fastify_ajv_pollution.description: Fastify AJV schema compilation reads $defs / $ref. Pollution of these via __proto__ poisons input validation across the application.

probes.description: Pollute AJV schema. Following requests with bodies hit a validation hang or 500

exec_argv_rce_chain.description: PortSwigger 2023 — Node child_process spawn picks up polluted execArgv across the runtime. Combined with `--experimental-vm-modules` toggle, leads to arbitrary code in spawned child. Use --inspect to confirm: presence of `Debugger listening on` in logs after pollution implies arbitrary --inspect flag accepted.

probes.description: Pollute execArgv. Next child_process.spawn / fork inherits --inspect flag pointing to attacker

hapi_event_pollution.description: Hapi server.events.emit reads from internal config that's prototype-walked. Polluted entries fire on every request.

probes.description: Pollute Hapi onRequest handler default. Next request 500s with internal handler missing

side_channel_status_delta.description: Generic detection — pollute Object.prototype.fooBarBaz with unique sentinel, then call sibling endpoint and grep response for the sentinel. If any code path enumerates `for (k in obj)` over a non-Object.create(null) object, sentinel will appear.

probes.description: Side-channel probe — sentinel reflected back in unrelated API response is confirmation
